ListItem.Para: Use of flaps 20 for takeoff is prohibited.
Landing gear remains extended for 10 minutes after takeoff.

Note.Para: In case of engine failure after V1, landing gear should be retracted until takeoff obstacles are cleared and airplane is 1500 feet above the airport.

ListItem.Para: Increase flight planning fuel by 2,900 lb (1,315 kg).
Use of flaps 20 for takeoff is prohibited.
After takeoff, leave the landing gear extended for a minimum of ten minutes.

In case of engine failure after V1, landing gear should be retracted after takeoff until a minimum of 1500ft. Extending gear after engine failure can cause the airplane to descend. Extend the gear after terrain clearance is assured.
It is not necessary to apply landing gear extended takeoff performance adjustments when dispatching under this item.
Increase flight planning fuel by 2,800 lb (1,270 kg).
Use of flaps 20 for takeoff is prohibited.
After takeoff, leave the landing gear extended for a minimum of ten minutes.

In case of engine failure after V1, landing gear should be retracted after takeoff until a minimum of 1500ft. Extending gear after engine failure can cause the airplane to descend. Extend the gear after terrain clearance is assured.
It is not necessary to apply landing gear extended takeoff performance adjustments when dispatching under this item.

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: One detector per wheel axle may be inoperative provided outboard detectors operate normally.

MaintenanceNote.Para: Outboard detectors and associated status messages are shown in the following table:
Detectors 1, 2 and 3 are associated with the forward axle of the landing gear truck and detectors 4, 5 and 6 are associated with the rear axle of the landing gear truck.
Wheel well fire temperature detectors are interchangeable.
